title: OVH keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: OVH Application Key detected in variable assignment. OVH API keys authenticate access to OVH cloud infrastructure including servers, storage, networking, and domain management. Exposing these credentials allows unauthorized access to OVH resources, enabling attackers to provision services, modify configurations, access data, and incur significant costs. Revoke this key immediately from OVH control panel and use environment variables or secret management systems for secure storage.

OVH Application Secret detected in variable assignment. OVH Application Secret is a critical credential that pairs with the Application Key to authenticate API requests. Exposing this secret compromises the entire OVH API authentication, allowing unauthorized control over cloud infrastructure, virtual machines, storage, DNS, and billing. Rotate this secret immediately in OVH API dashboard and implement secure credential storage.

OVH Consumer Key detected in variable assignment. OVH Consumer Key authenticates user/application access to specific OVH resources and services. Exposing this key enables unauthorized API operations on behalf of the consumer, including resource provisioning, configuration changes, data access, and potential service disruption. Revoke this key from OVH API management panel and use secure credential storage.

Generic OVH API key detected in source code (32-character format). OVH API keys provide access to cloud infrastructure management including compute instances, dedicated servers, storage solutions, networking, DNS management, and billing operations. Exposing these credentials in code enables unauthorized access to OVH resources, service modifications, data breaches, and financial losses from resource consumption. Use environment variables or dedicated secret managers and rotate all exposed keys immediately.
